passages.title: Global Warming in New Zealand 2
passages.source_html:
Global Warming in New Zealand 2 


A 


New Zealand is expected to warm by about 3℃ over the next century. The northern polar regions will be more than 6℃ warmer, while the large continents – also the largest centres of population – will be 4℃ or warmer. In contrast, the Southern Ocean, which surrounds New Zealand, may warm by only 2°C. The sea will act as an air conditioner and in this aspect, New Zealand’s location is comparatively fortunate. 


B 


Any predictions are complicated by the variability of New Zealand’s climate. The annual temperature can fluctuate as much as 1°C above or below the long-term average. The early summer of 2006-2007, for instance, was notably cool, thanks in part to the iceberg that drifted up the east coast. A few months later, warm water from the Tasman Sea helped make May 2007 unusually hot. These variables will continue unaffected so that, although the general pattern will be for rising temperatures, the warming trend may not be uniform. 


C 


The Ocean to the south of New Zealand will have one important effect. As the world warms, the great band of west winds that circle Antarctica will become stronger. This has already been observed, and its impact on New Zealand is likely to be profound, stronger, more frequent west winds will bring increased, sometimes catastrophic rainfall to the west coast of the country and create drier conditions in some eastern regions that are already drought-prone. At the same time, the general warming will spread south. 


D 


Furthermore, in the drier regions, the average moisture deficit – that is,the difference between the amount of water in soils available to plants and the amount plants need for optimum growth – will increase. Soils could go into moisture deficit earlier in the growing season and the deficits could last longer into autumn than at present. What we think of today as a medium-severity drought could be an almost annual occurrence by the end of the century. One direct consequence of warmer – and shorter – winters will be a reduction in snow cover. The permanent snow line in the mountains will rise, while snow cover below this will be shorter-lived. The amount of snow that falls may actually increase, however, even in some northern centres, owing to the intensification of precipitation. Ski-field base station may eventually have to be moved upwards to be within reach of the new snow line but there could still be plenty of the white stuff up there. 


E 


There will also be a marked impact on New Zealand’s glaciers. Over the last 100 years, the glaciers have been reduced by 35%, although since 1978 increase snowfall has offset the effect of warming. The latest studies conducted by the National Institute for Water and Atmospheric. Research (NIWA), however, suggest that by the end of the century, warming over the Southern Alps could be significantly greater than over the rest of the country. 


F 


Sea levels around New Zealand have risen by 25cm since the middle of the 9th century and by 7 cm since 1990. Predictions for the coming years cover a wide range, however, partly because of unknown rises resulting from the melting of the ice in the Arctic, Greenland and Antarctica. In addition, sea level at any given time is affected by many different factors, one of which is called a storm surge. When a storm coincides with a high tide along low lying coastal areas, this bulge raises the tide higher than normal, creating a surge unlike a slow-motion tsunami, not only does a rise in sea level increase the potential for this sort of damage, but it also has less immediate impacts. One potentially grave outcome is that groundwater systems may become contaminated with saltwater, spoiling them for the irrigation of farmland, which in turn could diminish crop harvests. Similarly, overtime, estuaries may be enlarged by erosion as tidal influences reach further upstream, altering the contours of whole shorelines and initiating further unforeseen consequences. 


G 


The impacts these changes will have on New Zealand are difficult to generalize. Human systems are better able to adapt to change than natural ecosystems because humans can see a problem coming and plan a response. Farmers and horticulturalists have made considerable advances, replacing crops they grow to better suit the new conditions. However, plant breeders will need to show considerable ingenuity if they can overcome the acute water shortages that are forecast. 


H 


For natural ecosystems the rate of change is crucial. If it is low, the plants and animals and fish will be able to ‘keep up’; if it is high, only the most adaptable species-those that can survive in the widest range of ecological niches-are likely to survive. Species adapted to only a narrow range of conditions or food sources will find adaptation much more difficult. Take tuatara, for instance. Their sex is determined by the temperature at which the eggs are incubated in warm (currently above 22℃) condition become predominately male – and now males already outnumber females by nearly two to one in some island refuges. In the mountains, as the permanent snow line moves upwards, the tolerance zones of some alpine plants and animals may simply disappear. It should also be remembered that global warming is just that – a global phenomenon.'New Zealand's own greenhouse emissions are tiny – around 0.5% of the global total. Even if New Zealanders were to achieve the government’s target of carbon neutrality, this would have no discernable impact on global climate change. 


I 


The changes that global warming is going to bring to New Zealand during the 21st century are going to be significant, but where the country is likely to be most vulnerable is with respect to climate change elsewhere. New Zealand may warm more slowly than most places, but if its major export markets undergo damaging change, the economic impact will be severe.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27-32 


Choose the correct answer.


27

What is the main idea of the first paragraph?

正确答案： D. New Zealand will not warm as much as other countries in the next century because it is surrounded by sea.

原文答案句：New Zealand is expected to warm by about 3℃ over the next century. The northern polar regions will be more than 6℃ warmer, while the large continents – also the largest centres of population – will be 4℃ or warmer. In contrast, the Southern Ocean, which surrounds New Zealand, may warm by only 2°C. The sea will act as an air conditioner and in this aspect, New Zealand’s location is comparatively fortunate.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是第一段的主要思想是什么。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"the main idea of the first paragraph"定位到A段，该段主要讨论了新西兰在未来一个世纪的气候变化情况。

解题思路：根据A段的内容，新西兰预计在未来一个世纪内气温上升约3℃，而北极地区将上升超过6℃，大陆地区将上升4℃或更多。相比之下，环绕新西兰的南大洋可能只上升2℃。海洋将起到空调的作用，在这方面，新西兰的位置相对幸运。因此，A段的主要思想是新西兰由于被海洋环绕，在未来一个世纪内不会像其他国家那样升温那么多。所以正确答案是D。


28

What does the writer say about New Zealand's variable weather?

正确答案： B. Variable weather will continue, unchanged by global warming.

原文答案句：These variables will continue unaffected so that, although the general pattern will be for rising temperatures, the warming trend may not be uniform.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问作者关于新西兰多变的天气说了什么。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"New Zealand’s variable weather"定位到B段，该段讨论了新西兰气候的变异性。

解题思路：根据原文答案句可知，这些变量（指天气变异性）将继续不受影响，因此尽管总体模式是气温上升，但变暖趋势可能不会均匀。这与选项B"多变的天气将继续，不受全球变暖的影响"完全一致。其他选项A、C、D在原文中均未提及或与原文内容不符。


29

What is the predicted impact of conditions in the ocean to the south of New Zealand?

正确答案： A. New Zealand will be more affected by floods and droughts.

原文答案句：As the world warms, the great band of west winds that circle Antarctica will become stronger. This has already been observed, and its impact on New Zealand is likely to be profound, stronger, more frequent west winds will bring increased, sometimes catastrophic rainfall to the west coast of the country and create drier conditions in some eastern regions that are already drought-prone.  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问的是“预计海洋条件会对新西兰南部造成什么影响”。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“conditions in the ocean to the south of New Zealand”定位到C段，该段明确提到海洋条件的变化对新西兰南部的影响。  

解题思路：C段提到，随着全球变暖，环绕南极洲的西风带会变得更强，这将给新西兰带来更强烈、更频繁的西风，导致西海岸降雨增加，东部干旱地区变得更加干燥。这与选项A“新西兰将更多地受到洪水和干旱的影响”完全一致，因此A是正确答案。


30

The writer mentions ‘moisture deficit’ to show

正确答案： C. how growing conditions will deteriorate.

原文答案句：In the drier regions, the average moisture deficit – that is,the difference between the amount of water in soils available to plants and the amount plants need for optimum growth – will increase. Soils could go into moisture deficit earlier in the growing season and the deficits could last longer into autumn than at present. What we think of today as a medium-severity drought could be an almost annual occurrence by the end of the century.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问作者提到“水分赤字”是为了说明什么。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的“moisture deficit”定位到D段，找到相关句子。

解题思路：原文提到在更干燥的地区，水分赤字会增加，土壤会在生长季节更早出现水分赤字，赤字持续时间会更长。现在被认为是中等严重程度的干旱到本世纪末可能几乎每年都会发生。这说明水分赤字的出现会导致生长条件恶化，因为植物可用的水分减少，干旱更加频繁和严重。因此，作者提到“水分赤字”是为了说明生长条件将恶化，对应选项C。


31

What are the implications of global warming for New Zealand’s skiing industry

正确答案： D. warming may provide more snow for some ski locations.

原文答案句：The amount of snow that falls may actually increase, however, even in some northern centres, owing to the intensification of precipitation.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问全球变暖对新西兰的滑雪产业有什么影响。

定位关键信息：根据题干中的关键词"skiing"和"global warming"定位到D段，该段提到了滑雪场和降雪量的变化。

解题思路：原文明确提到"the amount of snow that falls may actually increase"，即降雪量可能会增加，这与选项D" warming may provide more snow for some ski locations"完全一致。其他选项A、B、C都与原文内容不符，原文并未提到滑雪场会降低海拔、滑雪季节会推迟或北方滑雪场会南迁。因此，正确答案是D。


32

The writer refers to NIWA’s latest studies in the paragraph E to show

正确答案： A. how a particular place could be affected by warming.

原文答案句：The latest studies conducted by the National Institute for Water and Atmospheric. Research (NIWA), however, suggest that by the end of the century, warming over the Southern Alps could be significantly greater than over the rest of the country.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问作者在第三段引用NIWA的最新研究是为了说明什么。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"NIWA’s latest studies"和"3rd paragraph"。根据文章段落顺序，E段是第三段，段落中提到了NIWA的最新研究，因此定位到E段。

解题思路：原文中E段提到NIWA的最新研究表明，到本世纪末，南阿尔卑斯山的变暖程度可能显著高于该国其他地区。这说明作者引用该研究是为了说明一个特定地区（南阿尔卑斯山）可能如何受到变暖的影响。这与选项A的描述一致，即"how a particular place could be affected by warming." 其他选项B、C、D的内容在原文中并未提及或与原文不符。


Questions 33-35 


Complete the summary using the list of words, A-G, below. Choose the correct word and move it into the gap.

Options (Word Bank):
A. agriculture production
B. tropical waters
C. tidal waves
D. polar regions
E. global warming
F. coastal land
G. high tides
33

Rising sea levels 

The extent of future sea level rises around New Zealand is uncertain and may be determined in the 33  .


正确答案： D

原文答案句：The extent of future sea level rises around New Zealand is uncertain and may be determined in the polar regions.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是未来海平面上升的程度在新西兰周围是不确定的，可能由什么决定。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"sea level rises"和"New Zealand"定位到F段第一句话，其中提到了海平面上升的不确定性以及可能由极地地区的冰融化导致。

解题思路：根据原文F段第一句话"The extent of future sea level rises around New Zealand is uncertain and may be determined in the polar regions."可以确定，未来海平面上升的程度在新西兰周围是不确定的，可能由极地地区的冰融化导致。因此，答案为D。


34

Another variable is sudden rises in sea level caused by bad weather. Higher sea levels can lead to reduced 34 

正确答案： A

原文答案句：Unlike a slow-motion tsunami, not only does a rise in sea level increase the potential for this sort of damage, but it also has less immediate impacts. One potentially grave outcome is that groundwater systems may become contaminated with saltwater, spoiling them for the irrigation of farmland, which in turn could diminish crop harvests. Similarly, overtime, estuaries may be enlarged by erosion as tidal influences reach further upstream, altering the contours of whole shorelines and initiating further unforeseen consequences.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“Another variable is sudden rises in sea level caused by bad weather. Higher sea levels can lead to reduced”，即另一种变量是恶劣天气引起的海平面突然上升，更高的海平面会导致什么减少。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词“sudden rises in sea level caused by bad weather”和“Higher sea levels can lead to reduced”，在文章中定位到F段，其中提到了“rise in sea level”和“diminish crop harvests”。

解题思路：在F段中，原文提到“groundwater systems may become contaminated with saltwater, spoiling them for the irrigation of farmland, which in turn could diminish crop harvests.”，即海平面上升会导致地下水系统被盐水污染，从而影响农田灌溉，进而减少农作物产量。因此，题目中的空格应填入与“crop harvests”相对应的答案。根据选项，正确答案为A。



35

and result in changes to the shape of 35  .

正确答案： F

原文答案句：Similarly, overtime, estuaries may be enlarged by erosion as tidal influences reach further upstream, altering the contours of whole shorelines and initiating further unforeseen consequences.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是全球变暖导致海平面上升，进而对海岸线的形状产生什么变化。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"changes to the shape of"定位到F段末句，该句提到海平面上升会导致"altering the contours of whole shorelines"。

解题思路：原文中的"altering the contours of whole shorelines"与题目中的"changes to the shape of"意思一致，"contours of whole shorelines"指的是整个海岸线的轮廓或形状，因此答案为F。



Questions 36-40 


Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage 3? In boxes 36-40 on your answer sheet, write YES if the statement is trueNO if the statement is false NOT GIVEN if the information is not given in the passage


36

The natural world is less responsive to challenges than humans.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：Human systems are better able to adapt to change than natural ecosystems because humans can see a problem coming and plan a response.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是“自然世界对挑战的应对不如人类”是否与文章作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“natural world”和“humans”可以定位到G段的第一句话。

解题思路：原文中明确提到“Human systems are better able to adapt to change than natural ecosystems”，即人类系统比自然生态系统更能适应变化。这与题目中的表述“自然世界对挑战的应对不如人类”意思一致，因此答案为A（YES）。


37

The agricultural sector is being too conservative and resistant to innovation.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：Farmers and horticulturalists have made considerable advances, replacing crops they grow to better suit the new conditions.  

对应段落：G段  

审题：题目问农业部门是否过于保守且抵制创新。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"agricultural sector"（农业部门）和"conservative and resistant to innovation"（保守且抵制创新）定位到G段。  

解题思路：原文提到农民和园艺家已经取得了显著进展，他们更换了农作物以适应新条件，说明农业部门正在积极适应变化并进行创新，而不是保守或抵制创新。因此，题干的说法与原文相反，应选择"NO"。


38

Global warming is slow; it will affect different regions in different ways.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：The Ocean to the south of New Zealand will have one important effect. As the world warms, the great band of west winds that circle Antarctica will become stronger. This has already been observed, and its impact on New Zealand is likely to be profound, stronger, more frequent west winds will bring increased, sometimes catastrophic rainfall to the west coast of the country and create drier conditions in some eastern regions that are already drought-prone. At the same time, the general warming will spread south.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问“全球变暖是缓慢的；它将以不同的方式影响不同的地区。”是否与文章作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“global warming”和“different regions in different ways”定位到C段，该段提到全球变暖对不同地区的影响不同。

解题思路：文章C段提到全球变暖对不同地区的影响不同，但并未提及全球变暖是缓慢的。因此，题目中的“全球变暖是缓慢的”这一信息在文章中没有明确提及，无法判断其是否与作者观点一致。故答案为NOT GIVEN。


39

The tuatara is vulnerable to changes in climate conditions.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：Their sex is determined by the temperature at which the eggs are incubated in warm (currently above 22℃) condition become predominately male – and now males already outnumber females by nearly two to one in some island refuges.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问“tuatara易受气候变化的影响”是否与作者观点一致。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“tuatara”和“vulnerable to changes in climate conditions”定位到H段，该段明确提到tuatara的性别由孵化温度决定，且高温会导致雄性比例增加，说明其易受气候影响。

解题思路：原文指出tuatara的性别受温度影响，且高温环境下雄性数量已超过雌性，直接说明其易受气候变化影响，因此题目陈述与作者观点一致，答案为YES。


40

New Zealand must reduce carbon emission if global warming is to be slowed.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：New Zealand’s own greenhouse emissions are tiny – around 0.5% of the global total. Even if New Zealanders were to achieve the government’s target of carbon neutrality, this would have no discernable impact on global climate change.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问“如果全球变暖要被减缓，新西兰必须减少碳排放”是否与作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“New Zealand”、“reduce carbon emission”和“slow global warming”定位到H段，其中提到新西兰的碳排放量很小，即使实现碳中和也对全球气候变暖没有明显影响。

解题思路：原文明确指出新西兰的碳排放量仅占全球总量的0.5%，即使完全实现碳中和也无法对全球气候变暖产生显著影响。因此，题目中“新西兰必须减少碳排放以减缓全球变暖”的说法与原文矛盾，作者并未支持这一观点。答案为NO。
